title: utricularia vulgaris subsp. macrorhiza
intro: Utricularia vulgaris subsp. macrorhiza, also known as the greater bladderwort, is a carnivorous aquatic plant belonging to the bladderwort family (Lentibulariaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Utricularia vulgaris subsp. macrorhiza is often used as a tea, tincture, or syrup, although its use should be under the guidance of a medical professional due to potential side effects.
